title: Noisy alert triage
excerpt: Check alert repetition and user impact before escalating.
steps: Check whether the alert repeated in at least two windows.
Compare the alert with user-impacting latency, error, and availability metrics.
Look for recovery-on-retry logs before opening remediation work.
Route to owner review only when impact is unclear or the alert keeps flapping.
validation_signals: single synthetic probe failure recovered on retry
safe_actions: Ask the owner to confirm whether the alert is actionable.
Attach evidence to the CDO handoff without creating remediation work automatically.
avoid: Do not page critical on-call for a single recovered probe failure.
Do not recommend rollback when no deploy correlation exists.

escalation.reason: Only escalate if weak signals repeat or user-impacting metrics degrade.

title: Synthetic probe false-positive review
excerpt: Use when a synthetic probe times out once but service-level metrics remain healthy.
steps: Check probe location, retry success, and recent probe configuration changes.
Compare synthetic failure with real user monitoring or service metrics.
Suppress duplicate notifications only through the monitoring owner workflow.
Document why the incident remains investigation-only.
validation_signals: no matching service logs at error level
safe_actions: Keep status as INVESTIGATE unless impact evidence appears.
Recommend alert tuning review rather than remediation.
avoid: Do not create a production incident without corroborating evidence.

escalation.reason: Escalate only when probe noise becomes sustained or impacts users.
